assigned.summaryHtml:
This event occurs when there is activity relating to a secret scanning alert. For more information about secret scanning, see "About secret scanning." For information about the API to manage secret scanning alerts, see "Secret scanning" in the REST API documentation.

For activity relating to secret scanning alert locations, use the secret_scanning_alert_location event.

To subscribe to this event, a GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the "Secret scanning alerts" repository permission.

childParamsGroups.name: number
childParamsGroups.description: The security alert number.

childParamsGroups.name: created_at
childParamsGroups.description: The time that the alert was created in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

childParamsGroups.name: url
childParamsGroups.description: The REST API URL of the alert resource.

childParamsGroups.name: html_url
childParamsGroups.description: The GitHub URL of the alert resource.

childParamsGroups.name: locations_url
childParamsGroups.description: The REST API URL of the code locations for this alert.

childParamsGroups.name: resolution
childParamsGroups.description: The reason for resolving the alert.

childParamsGroups.name: resolved_at
childParamsGroups.description: The time that the alert was resolved in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

childParamsGroups.name: resolved_by
childParamsGroups.description: A GitHub user.

childParamsGroups.name: resolution_comment
childParamsGroups.description: An optional comment to resolve an alert.

childParamsGroups.name: secret_type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of secret that secret scanning detected.

childParamsGroups.name: secret_type_display_name
childParamsGroups.description: User-friendly name for the detected secret, matching the secret_type. For a list of built-in patterns, see "Supported secret scanning patterns."

childParamsGroups.name: validity
childParamsGroups.description: The token status as of the latest validity check.

childParamsGroups.name: push_protection_bypassed
childParamsGroups.description: Whether push protection was bypassed for the detected secret.

childParamsGroups.name: push_protection_bypassed_by
childParamsGroups.description: A GitHub user.

childParamsGroups.name: push_protection_bypassed_at
childParamsGroups.description: The time that push protection was bypassed in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

childParamsGroups.name: push_protection_bypass_request_reviewer
childParamsGroups.description: A GitHub user.

childParamsGroups.name: push_protection_bypass_request_reviewer_comment
childParamsGroups.description: An optional comment when reviewing a push protection bypass.

childParamsGroups.name: push_protection_bypass_request_comment
childParamsGroups.description: An optional comment when requesting a push protection bypass.

childParamsGroups.name: push_protection_bypass_request_html_url
childParamsGroups.description: The URL to a push protection bypass request.

childParamsGroups.name: multi_repo
childParamsGroups.description: Whether the detected secret was found in multiple repositories in the same organization or business.

childParamsGroups.name: assigned_to
childParamsGroups.description: A GitHub user.

bodyParameters.name: assignee
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub user.

bodyParameters.name: enterprise
bodyParameters.description: An enterprise on GitHub. Webhook payloads contain the enterprise property when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account or an organization that's part of an enterprise account. For more information, see "About enterprise accounts."

bodyParameters.name: installation
bodyParameters.description: The GitHub App installation. Webhook payloads contain the installation property when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App. For more information, see "Using webhooks with GitHub Apps."

bodyParameters.name: organization
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub organization. Webhook payloads contain the organization property when the webhook is configured for an organization, or when the event occurs from activity in a repository owned by an organization.

bodyParameters.name: repository
bodyParameters.description: The repository on GitHub where the event occurred. Webhook payloads contain the repository property when the event occurs from activity in a repository.

bodyParameters.name: sender
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub user.

reopened.descriptionHtml: A previously closed secret scanning alert was reopened.
